a:Ta113,<div class="question">
<p>Unpolarized light with an intensity of 320 W m<sup>−2</sup> goes through a polarizer and an analyser, originally aligned parallel.</p>
<p><img src="data:image/png;base64," style="display:block;margin-left:auto;margin-right:auto;"/></p>
<p>The analyser is rotated through an angle <em>θ</em> = 30°. Cos 30° = <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><msqrt><mn>3</mn></msqrt><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math>.</p>
<p><img src="data:image/png;base64," style="display:block;margin-left:auto;margin-right:auto;"/></p>
<p>What is the intensity of the light emerging from the analyser? </p>
<p>A.  120 W m<sup>−2</sup></p>
<p>B.  <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>80</mn><msqrt><mn>3</mn></msqrt></math> W m<sup>−2</sup></p>
<p>C.  240 W m<sup>−2</sup></p>
<p>D.  <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>160</mn><msqrt><mn>3</mn></msqrt></math> W m<sup>−2</sup></p>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/physics/topic/4/","children":["← back to ","Physics"," topic ","4"]}]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"22M.1.SL.TZ2.18"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"tag","children":"pestle"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Physics"}],null,["$","span",null,{"children":"SL"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["Paper ","1"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"22M"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["· ","4-3-wave-characteristics"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"https://pestle.pages.dev/app/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener","children":"source ↗"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-content","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$a"}}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Markscheme / solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\">\n<p>A</p>\n</div>"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\">\n<p>This question proved a little challenging for SL candidates with many choosing incorrect answers especially C or D instead of correct A. At HL option C also was a popular option although a high discrimination index shows that the question discriminated well between the candidates. It appears that candidates are forgetting that 50% of the intensity is lost when unpolarised light passes through a polarizer, and then more is lost at the analyser according to Malus' Law.</p>\n</div>"}}]]}]]}]
